_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
The Queensland Legislation Updates — May 2026 package spans multiple regulatory domains, none of which directly impose freight-forwarder-specific obligations in the manner of, say, a customs or transport safety statute. However, the workspace sits within this legislative environment in the following ways:

**Biosecurity Regulation 2016 (under review — Biosecurity Act 2014):** The workspace functions as a 'carrier' within the meaning of the Biosecurity Act 2014. The Act imposes a General Biosecurity Obligation (GBO) on all persons — including logistics operators — to take all reasonable and practical measures to minimise biosecurity risks posed by their activities. This applies to movements of cargo, equipment, vehicles, soil, and biological material in, out of, or within Queensland. The Biosecurity Regulation 2016 prescribes statewide entry restrictions, biosecurity zone conditions (fire ant, varroa mite, banana, cattle tick, electric ant, polyphagous shot-hole borer zones), and livestock traceability requirements that directly affect road freight movements in Queensland. The workspace must confirm routing does not breach zone movement restrictions and that drivers/operators are aware of reporting obligations for prohibited and restricted matter. *Source: Biosecurity Act 2014, Queensland Parliament, commenced 1 July 2016; Biosecurity Regulation 2016 (current); DPI Queensland, 2026. https://www.dpi.qld.gov.au/business-priorities/biosecurity/policy-legislation-regulation/biosecurity-act-2014*

**Trusts Act 2025:** The workspace's role here is indirect — as a counterparty or agent to clients whose assets, consignments, or legal structures may be held in trust. For the workspace's own operational trust structures (if any) in Queensland, it is a regulated trustee subject to codified duties and expanded record-keeping obligations. Legal confirmation is required as to whether any Queensland-registered trust arrangement under the workspace's operational control is captured. *Source: Trusts Act 2025, Queensland Parliamentary Counsel, commenced 28 April 2026. https://www.legislation.qld.gov.au/view/whole/html/inforce/2026-05-02/act-2025-008*

**Regional Planning Interests (Condamine Alluvium) and Other Legislation Amendment Bill 2026:** The workspace is not a direct duty-holder under this Bill. Its relevance is operational — cargo movements supporting CSG or agricultural operations in the Condamine Alluvium area (Surat Basin, Western Downs, Southern Downs, Toowoomba) may be affected by changes to approvals, compensation regimes, and land-access arrangements for petroleum resource authority holders. The workspace should monitor whether clients operating in this region face access-land disruptions post-enactment. Legal confirmation of the workspace's exposure as a logistics provider to resource authority holders is recommended. *Source: Regional Planning Interests (Condamine Alluvium) and Other Legislation Amendment Bill 2026, Queensland Parliament, introduced 25 March 2026. https://www.planning.qld.gov.au/planning-framework/legislation/regional-planning-interests-condamine-alluvium-and-other-legislation-amendment-bill-2026*
